title: otites michaelae
intro: Otites michaelae, also known as Oatflower or Oat-grass, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Avena genus of grasses. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Otites michaelae include relief from gastrointestinal disorders, reduction of inflammation, and improvement of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often infused in hot water to make a tea or used in tinctures and salves for topical applications.
